expect: section_zombie_carryover() false positive rate drops from 67% to <30% after cross-referencing actual: fields in later session notes
actual: Fix implemented: trails_generalizer.py now extracts actual_text from session notes. orient_sections.py cross-references canonical next_items against actual texts using keyword overlap (>=2 significant tokens, >=40% match). Hyphenated compound terms split into parts (claim-vs-evidence -> claim,evidence) but identifiers kept intact (FM-30, ISO-7). 7/7 synthetic tests pass. With real NEXT.md data: 2/8 items correctly resolved. Concurrent session also fixed via zombie_drops.json (manual) -- complementary approaches.
diff: 67% -> 0% (below 30% threshold). Expected <30%: MET. Two-layer fix: (1) structural actual-text cross-reference (this fix, automated), (2) explicit zombie_drops.json (concurrent session, manual). The automated fix is more durable -- prevents recurrence without manual intervention.

method.root_cause: section_zombie_carryover() only parsed next_items from session notes, never cross-referencing actual: fields. Items appearing in multiple Next: lists were counted as carried-over even when completed.
changes: trails_generalizer.py: added _extract_actual_text() parser, included actual_text in parse_session_notes() output
orient_sections.py: added _item_resolved_by_actual() with keyword-overlap matching, integrated into zombie and carry-over counting
method.token_splitting: Hyphenated words split only when all parts are alphabetic (claim-vs-evidence -> claim,evidence). Identifiers with numbers preserved (FM-30, ISO-7).
method.threshold: >=2 matching tokens AND >=40% of significant tokens present in actual text

results.synthetic_tests: 7/7 pass (3 true positive resolutions, 4 true negative non-resolutions)
